chief_complaint: A 58-year-old female presented with upper abdominal pain for 3 days.
history: The patient had a history of diabetes and presented with upper abdominal pain. No other specific past medical history, medications, or social history were reported.
exam: No specific vital signs or physical examination findings were reported.
labs: Fasting blood glucose was 21 mmol/L, urinary ketone body 3+, pH 7.309, decreased actual and standard bicarbonate, and abdominal enhanced computed tomography showed a rounded low-density shadow with sharp edges and well-defined borders in the lumen of the bowel on the left side of the abdomen, with a size of 6 cm, and no significant enhancement within the lesion.
correct_diagnosis: Small intestinal obstruction caused by bezoar combined with diabetes ketoacidosis
differentials: Small intestinal obstruction caused by bezoar combined with diabetes ketoacidosis
correct_ranking: Small intestinal obstruction caused by bezoar combined with diabetes ketoacidosis
